evals.prompt: I want a self-hosted, private alternative to Google NotebookLM that I run on my own machine and can drive programmatically. How do I stand it up and organize my research into notebooks?
evals.expected_output: Triggers alterlab-open-notebook. Recommends Open Notebook, the self-hosted open-source NotebookLM alternative with a full REST API. Describes Docker Compose deployment (setting OPEN_NOTEBOOK_ENCRYPTION_KEY, docker-compose up), the UI at localhost:8502 and REST API at localhost:5055, and creating a notebook via POST /api/notebooks.

assertions.value: Mentions self-hosted deployment (Docker Compose / encryption key) and creating a notebook via the REST API.

evals.prompt: I've collected a bunch of arXiv links and PDFs for my tumor mutational burden review. I want to load them all into one workspace and then ask an AI questions that cite the actual sources. What's the workflow?
evals.expected_output: Triggers alterlab-open-notebook. Explains ingesting sources via POST /api/sources (URLs and uploaded PDF files, with vector + full-text indexing), then context-aware chat via /api/chat/sessions and /api/chat/execute with context including sources so answers cite them. May mention search (/api/search vector or full-text).

assertions.value: Describes ingesting sources into a notebook and using context-aware chat (or search) that cites those sources.

evals.prompt: Can I turn my collected research materials into a podcast episode with three different speakers discussing the findings, all generated locally?
evals.expected_output: Triggers alterlab-open-notebook. Explains Open Notebook's multi-speaker podcast generation (1-4 customizable speakers, beyond NotebookLM's 2-speaker limit) via POST /api/podcasts/generate with an episode profile and speaker profile IDs, polling the job status, and downloading the episode audio.

assertions.value: Describes generating a multi-speaker podcast from research materials via the /api/podcasts generate-and-poll workflow.

evals.prompt: I need my document-analysis notebook to stay fully private with no API costs — can I run it against a local Ollama model instead of a cloud provider, and configure that through the API?
evals.expected_output: Triggers alterlab-open-notebook. Confirms Open Notebook supports 16+ AI providers including Ollama for free local inference and full data sovereignty via self-hosting. Describes configuring a provider/credential (POST /api/credentials), discovering and registering models, and that data stays in local Docker volumes.

assertions.value: Confirms local/private inference via Ollama and configuring the provider through credentials/models in the REST API.

evals.prompt: I just need this one PowerPoint deck converted to clean Markdown text so I can paste it into a prompt — nothing fancy, no database or chat, just the file as Markdown.
evals.expected_output: Should NOT trigger alterlab-open-notebook — there's no notebook, ingestion, chat, search, or podcast involved. A one-shot PPTX-to-Markdown conversion is alterlab-markitdown's job (Microsoft MarkItDown CLI/Python). Defer to alterlab-markitdown.
